title: BYOS: Remove agent filtering, inject all skills, add prefer-skills language
description:
Two changes to BYOS skill system:

1. REMOVE AGENT/PHASE FILTERING: All project skills in .claude/skills/*/SKILL.md should be discovered and injected into EVERY workflow step for EVERY agent. No more matching by agent or phase. The discover_prism_skills() function should scan and return ALL valid skills regardless of which agent/step is running. The prism.agent and prism.phase fields in SKILL.md frontmatter become optional/informational only (not used for filtering).

2. ADD PREFER-SKILLS LANGUAGE: Change the injection language from passive ('invoke if relevant') to explicit directive language. Agents should ALWAYS prefer using an available skill that can resolve their task rather than attempting to solve without one. Make this a clear instruction in build_agent_instruction output.

Files affected:
- hooks/prism_loop_context.py: discover_prism_skills() remove agent/phase params and filtering, _format_discovered_skills() update language, build_agent_instruction() inject skills unconditionally
- skills/byos/SKILL.md: Update docs to reflect agent is optional
- skills/byos/commands/byos.md: Update scaffold/validate usage examples
- skills/byos/scripts/scaffold_skill.py: Make --agent optional (no longer required for prism: block)
- skills/byos/scripts/validate_skill.py: Don't error on missing agent field
- skills/byos/reference/getting-started.md: Update docs
- skills/byos/reference/skill-template.md: Update template
- skills/byos/reference/examples.md: Update examples
- tests/test_prism_loop_context.py: Update tests for new discovery behavior

Acceptance criteria:
- discover_prism_skills() returns ALL skills from .claude/skills/ without filtering
- build_agent_instruction() injects all discovered skills into every step
- Injection text explicitly tells agent to prefer using skills over solving without them
- scaffold_skill.py works without --agent flag
- validate_skill.py does not require agent field
- All existing tests pass (updated as needed)
closeReason: BYOS skill system updated: removed agent/phase filtering from discover_prism_skills(), made agent field optional in _parse_skill_frontmatter(), added ALWAYS prefer directive language in _format_discovered_skills(), updated scaffold_skill.py and validate_skill.py for optional agent, updated all BYOS reference docs. 33/33 tests passing across 2 builders.

title: Fix Python 3.10 and WSL compatibility for PRISM plugin
description:
Two blocking issues prevent the PRISM plugin from running in WSL/Linux:

1. hooks.json uses 'python' command for all 6 hooks — WSL/Linux only has 'python3'. Need to change all hook commands to use 'python3' instead of 'python'.

2. setup_prism_loop.py has a Python 3.10 SyntaxError at line 255/256 — 'f-string expression part cannot include a backslash'. The triple-quoted f-string starting at line 227 fails to parse on Python 3.10.12 (WSL). Works on Python 3.12+ (Windows). Fix: refactor the f-string to avoid the construct Python 3.10 rejects. Also scan ALL other .py files in the plugin for similar Python 3.10 incompatibilities.

Acceptance: All plugin Python files parse cleanly under Python 3.10. hooks.json uses python3. Existing tests pass.
closeReason: Fixed Python 3.10 and WSL compatibility: (1) hooks.json all 6 commands changed python->python3, (2) setup_prism_loop.py extracted backslash expression from f-string for Python 3.10 compat. All plugin .py files scan clean.

title: TUI _read_live_tokens silently failing - no tokens or model in dashboard
description:
CRITICAL BUG: The TUI dashboard (textual app mode) does not show tokens or model. The snapshot mode (--snapshot) works perfectly showing 670.9k tokens and claude-opus-4-6 model. Both use equivalent transcript-reading logic but the TUI _read_live_tokens in app.py is silently failing.

SYMPTOMS:
- TUI header shows: PRISM Dashboard ●ACTIVE sess:697911d8 (NO model, NO tokens)
- Agent roster Tokens column shows: - -
- Snapshot shows: 670.9k tokens, model claude-opus-4-6, 15.8k tok/min

ROOT CAUSE CANDIDATES:
1. _read_live_tokens returns early before setting state.total_tokens (glob fails, file not found, etc)
2. An uncaught exception (not IOError/OSError) propagates and Textual silently swallows it
3. The glob pattern works in snapshot but fails in TUI context for unknown reason

FILES:
- plugins/prism-devtools/tools/prism-cli/app.py - _read_live_tokens method (lines 116-194)
- plugins/prism-devtools/tools/prism-cli/snapshot.py - _inject_live_tokens (working reference, lines 22-74)

- Transcript: /home/siegeon/.claude/projects/-tmp-prism-test/697911d8-0c3c-4f01-805b-5945d818f8d5.jsonl (243KB)

- Add error handling that doesnt silently swallow failures

closeReason: Fixed TUI _read_live_tokens silent failure. Root cause: Python for-in file iterator read-ahead buffer broke f.tell() offset tracking, plus missing guards for non-dict JSON entries and null token values. title: Fix token counting mismatch between stop hook and display
description: The stop hook get_usage_from_transcript() only sums input_tokens + output_tokens (2 fields), but the display code in snapshot.py and app.py sums input_tokens + cache_creation_input_tokens + cache_read_input_tokens + output_tokens (4 fields). This causes: (1) total_tokens in TIMING shows 7.5M but step rows don't add up, (2) step_tokens = total_tokens(4-field) - step_tokens_start(2-field) produces inflated garbage. Fix: add cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens to get_usage_from_transcript() in prism_stop_hook.py. Update existing tests to verify consistency.

title: CLI version: prefer CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT for plugin.json resolution
description:
The _read_plugin_version() in app.py and snapshot.py currently uses Path(__file__).parent... to find plugin.json. This should prefer the CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var (which points to the actually-loaded plugin, whether cached or source) and fall back to __file__-relative only if the env var is unset.

Files to modify:
1. plugins/prism-devtools/tools/prism-cli/app.py — _read_plugin_version() around line 31
2. plugins/prism-devtools/tools/prism-cli/snapshot.py — _read_plugin_version() around line 22

Change logic to:
  root = os.environ.get('CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT')
  if root:
      plugin_json = Path(root) / '.claude-plugin' / 'plugin.json'
  else:
      plugin_json = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / '.claude-plugin' / 'plugin.json'

This ensures the version shown is from the plugin Claude is actually running, not necessarily the source copy. Critical for detecting stale cache.

Acceptance criteria:
- Both files use CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT when available
- Falls back to __file__-relative when env var is unset
- Existing tests pass
- Update test to verify env var path is preferred
closeReason: Both app.py and snapshot.py _read_plugin_version() now prefer CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT env var for plugin.json resolution, with __file__-relative fallback. 2 new tests added. 140 tests passing.

title: BYOS: Fix skill invocation instruction — stop forcing all skills every step
description:
The _format_discovered_skills() function in prism_loop_context.py line 254 says 'MANDATORY: You MUST invoke each of the following skills... Do not skip any skill listed here'. This forces the agent to call ALL discovered skills on EVERY step, even when irrelevant. Evidence: session ad053fc6 shows 's':3 on every step — test-skill, no-prism-skill, and calculator called every single step.

Fix: Change line 254 to present skills as available-if-relevant, not mandatory-all. New wording should say skills are available and should be invoked if there is even a 1% chance they are relevant to the current task, but NOT forced unconditionally. The agent should decide based on relevance, with a very low threshold for calling.

File: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_loop_context.py line 254
Update tests if any exist for _format_discovered_skills.
closeReason: Changed _format_discovered_skills() line 254 from unconditional force-all-skills to relevance-gated 1% threshold. Updated test assertion. Builder confirmed 44/44 tests pass.

title: Brain auto-recovery on corrupt database
description:
GitHub issue #4 (siegeon/.prism): Brain fails to init when .prism/brain/*.db files are corrupted (sqlite 'database disk image is malformed'). brain_bootstrap() in setup_prism_loop.py catches the error silently and skips — Brain never initializes for the session.

Fix: Add corruption detection and auto-recovery to Brain.__init__ or brain_bootstrap():
1. In Brain.__init__, after connecting, run PRAGMA integrity_check on each db. If any returns non-'ok', raise a specific error (e.g. BrainCorruptError).
2. In brain_bootstrap(), catch that error, delete all .prism/brain/*.db files, and retry Brain() + ingest() once.
3. Alternative: do the detection/recovery entirely inside Brain.__init__ — if integrity_check fails, close connections, delete corrupt db files, reconnect fresh.

Files: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/brain_engine.py (Brain class __init__, _connect), plugins/prism-devtools/skills/prism-loop/scripts/setup_prism_loop.py (brain_bootstrap function).

Acceptance criteria:
- If brain.db or graph.db or scores.db is malformed, Brain auto-deletes and rebuilds
- brain_bootstrap() succeeds on second attempt after rebuild
- Existing tests still pass
- Add test for corruption recovery path

title: Fix FTS5 content-sync corruption in Brain ingest
description:
Root cause of GitHub issue #4: Brain.db FTS5 table uses content='docs' (external content table) but has NO sync triggers and uses wrong DELETE syntax, causing corruption on every ingest.

Problems in brain_engine.py:
1. _init_brain_schema() (L220-225): FTS5 table declared with content='docs', content_rowid='rowid' but no AFTER INSERT/UPDATE/DELETE triggers on docs table. SQLite FTS5 docs require these triggers for content tables.
2. _ingest_single() (L390): 'DELETE FROM docs_fts WHERE id = ?' is wrong for content tables. Must use: INSERT INTO docs_fts(docs_fts, rowid, id, content, domain) VALUES('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.content, old.domain)
3. _ingest_single() (L384-388): INSERT OR REPLACE on docs does hidden DELETE+INSERT, desynchronizing FTS5.

Fix options (choose one):
A. Add proper content-sync triggers (AFTER INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE on docs) and remove manual FTS5 DELETE/INSERT from _ingest_single. This is the SQLite-recommended approach.
B. Remove content='docs' from FTS5 table (let FTS5 store its own copy). Simpler but doubles storage.

Option A is preferred. Required triggers:
- AFTER INSERT ON docs: INSERT INTO docs_fts(rowid, id, content, domain) VALUES(new.rowid, new.id, new.content, new.domain)
- AFTER DELETE ON docs: INSERT INTO docs_fts(docs_fts, rowid, id, content, domain) VALUES('delete', old.rowid, old.id, old.content, old.domain)
- AFTER UPDATE ON docs: delete-old then insert-new pattern

Then in _ingest_single(), remove lines 390-394 (manual FTS5 sync) — the triggers handle it.

Also: the previous corruption recovery (BrainCorruptError + _check_db_integrity) should be KEPT as defense-in-depth, but it won't trigger anymore once this root cause is fixed.

Files: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/brain_engine.py (_init_brain_schema, _ingest_single)
Tests: plugins/prism-devtools/tests/test_brain_recovery.py (extend), add FTS5 ingest round-trip test

Acceptance:
- Brain().ingest() completes without DatabaseError on fresh db
- FTS5 search returns results after ingest (round-trip test)
- incremental_reindex() works without corruption
- Existing tests still pass
closeReason: Fixed FTS5 content-sync corruption in Brain ingest. Added AFTER INSERT/DELETE/UPDATE triggers for external content table sync. Removed manual FTS5 DELETE/INSERT from _ingest_single() and _remove_entries_by_source(). Added 5 new round-trip tests. All 11 tests pass.

3. In _cmd_init(): after ingest, print the mode (Full/Degraded) so user knows what they got. E.g. 'Brain: indexed 24 documents (mode: BM25+GraphRAG)' or 'Brain: indexed 24 documents (mode: Full — BM25+Vector+GraphRAG)'

title: Fix gate steps not blocking: systemMessage doesn't stop Claude
description:
GitHub issue #7: red_gate (step 5) doesn't stop Claude. The workflow skips from write_failing_tests directly to implement_tasks.

Root cause in prism_stop_hook.py:
- Line 970-972: Gate steps output {systemMessage: ...} which injects a message but does NOT block Claude from continuing
- Line 1013-1016: Agent steps output {decision: 'block', reason: ...} which actually stops Claude

Fix at line 970-972: Change gate output from systemMessage to decision:block format:
  print(json.dumps({
      'decision': 'block',
      'reason': f'[PRISM - Step {next_index + 1}/{len(WORKFLOW_STEPS)}: {next_step_id}]\n{gate_msg}'
  }))

This ensures Claude stops at gates and waits for /prism-approve or /prism-reject.

Also bump version 3.0.6 -> 3.0.7 in plugin.json and test_release_docs.py.

title: Conductor: implement epsilon-greedy prompt selection and brain relevance filtering
description: Conductor is a hollow shell — never calls best_prompt(), never does epsilon-greedy exploration, dumps all brain docs without relevance filtering. Pass prompt_id to prism_loop_context so it can use the variant text. (3) Add relevance threshold to system_context() — only include results with score above a minimum (e.g. 0.1) so it doesnt dump all docs. (4) Record the actual prompt_id used in record_outcome() so scores track which variant was used. Files: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/conductor_engine.py, plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/brain_engine.py (system_context relevance filter), plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_loop_context.py (accept prompt variant). title: Suppress HF Hub warning in Brain model2vec loading
description: GitHub issue #20. model2vec emits 'Warning: You are sending unauthenticated requests to the HF Hub' on every Brain command (init, status, search, etc). This clutters all output. Fix: suppress the warning at import/load time in brain_engine.py _load_model2vec() and _ensure_vector_deps(). Best approach: set os.environ['HF_HUB_DISABLE_IMPLICIT_TOKEN'] = '1' before importing model2vec, AND use warnings.filterwarnings('ignore', message='.*unauthenticated requests.*HF Hub.*') as a belt-and-suspenders approach. Do NOT require users to get an HF token — the model downloads fine without one, just with a noisy warning. The warning appears in both _load_model2vec() (line ~151) and the CLI auto-install section (line ~2306). Test: run 'python3 brain_engine.py status' and verify no HF warning appears in stdout or stderr. Add a unit test that imports the suppression and verifies the env var is set.
closeReason: Suppressed HF Hub unauthenticated-request warning in brain_engine.py via env var + warnings filter at module level, plus belt-and-suspenders in _load_model2vec(). Added 3 unit tests. All tests pass.

title: Brain _cli_source_dirs() expand to cover full project
description: GitHub issue #19. Brain _cli_source_dirs() in brain_engine.py (line ~2117) only scans hardcoded dirs: docs/, src/, lib/, scripts/, plugins/, hooks/. This misses critical sources: (1) root-level *.md files (CLAUDE.md, README.md, AGENTS.md), (2) .claude/skills/ where all skill docs live, (3) .prism/ handoff and memory files. Result: brain init indexes only 42 docs on a project with 2000+ indexable files. Fix: expand _cli_source_dirs() to also include: root-level *.md glob (Path.cwd().glob('*.md')), .claude/skills/ if it exists, .prism/ if it exists, and auto-detect additional source dirs via common patterns (app.*, packages/*, modules/*). Keep the _should_index() exclusions (.git, node_modules, __pycache__, etc) to filter junk. Also: the session-start hook (session-start.py) already calls Brain.incremental_reindex() — verify this uses the expanded source list too, not just the old hardcoded one. Test: add a unit test that mocks a project dir with .claude/skills/ and root *.md files, verify _cli_source_dirs() returns them.
closeReason: Expanded Brain _cli_source_dirs() to cover full project: root *.md files, .claude/skills/, .prism/brain/memory/, .prism/handoff.md, auto-detected dirs (app/packages/modules). Added _ALLOWED_SUBPATHS allowlist in _should_index() to selectively override excluded path segments. 13 new tests. 287/287 tests pass.

title: Stop hook: recursive globs hang on dotnet+node projects, blocking RED advancement
description:
GitHub issue #21. Stop hook at write_failing_tests step doesn't advance to red_gate despite tests being confirmed RED (9/10 fail). Root cause: two compounding issues in prism_stop_hook.py.

ROOT CAUSE 1 — PERFORMANCE: Recursive globs traverse node_modules, bin, obj directories.
- Line 70: detect_test_runner() uses cwd.glob('**/*.csproj') — recurses into node_modules with 50k+ files, hangs for minutes
- Lines 470-477: validate_step red_with_trace does cwd.glob('**/*Tests.cs'), cwd.glob('**/*.test.*') etc — same problem
- If hook exceeds Claude Code hook timeout, killed silently — no block/advance decision emitted
- Claude stops normally, state stays at write_failing_tests, next turn repeats — infinite loop burning tokens

ROOT CAUSE 2 — ERROR INDICATORS ARE PYTHON-ONLY: Line 434 error_indicators list (SyntaxError, ImportError, NameError, TypeError:) are all Python/Node patterns. Dotnet test output could contain 'NameError' or 'TypeError' in stack traces, causing false 'Tests have errors not assertions' block even when tests are correctly RED with assertions.

FIX REQUIRED:

title: Stop hook: validate_step times out running tests on large projects
description:
validate_step for red_with_trace/green calls run_tests() which spawns the full test suite. For large .NET/Aspire projects this easily exceeds Claude Code's hook timeout (~60s), silently killing the hook. No state advancement, no error message.

Root cause: run_tests() in the stop hook takes too long for projects with slow test suites (Docker, Aspire, integration tests).

Fix: 
1. Add transcript-based test result extraction as the primary validation path. The agent already ran tests during its turn — parse the transcript for test output instead of re-running.
2. Add timeout field to Stop hook in hooks.json (safety net).
3. Keep run_tests() as a fallback with reduced timeout if transcript check is inconclusive.

- plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/hooks.json — add timeout to Stop hook

- If transcript has test run results, uses those instead of re-running

title: Stop hook compaction resilience — detect no-progress stops and prevent double-advance
description:
After context compaction mid-workflow, the stop hook fires but can't distinguish meaningful step completion from a post-compaction idle stop. Three symptoms: (1) implement_tasks ran with Brain=0/Skills=0 because compaction lost PRISM context, (2) verify_green_state was skipped (double-advance), (3) green_gate never reached naturally — user had to manually edit state file.

Root cause: No 'no-progress' detection in the stop hook. When Claude stops after compaction without doing meaningful work for the current step, the hook still tries validation, which may pass on stale transcript data (test output from before compaction but after step_line_start), causing unwanted advancement.

Fix areas in prism_stop_hook.py:
1. Add no-progress detection: before validation, check if transcript has meaningful tool_use activity since step_line_start (e.g., minimum tool calls threshold for the current step type). If no progress detected, re-emit the current step instruction instead of validating/advancing.
2. Add step-transition guard: record the last-advanced-step timestamp and reject rapid re-advancement (debounce) to prevent double-advance.
3. Harden the transcript-based test extraction to not reuse stale test output — track test output line numbers and reject results older than the most recent compaction marker.

Files: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_stop_hook.py (main changes), plugins/prism-devtools/tests/test_prism_stop_hook.py (new tests)
GitHub: https://github.com/siegeon/.prism/issues/27
closeReason: Stop hook compaction resilience implemented: (1) no-progress detection blocks stop and re-emits current step when tool_calls below threshold, (2) step-transition debounce prevents double-advance on rapid re-stops with low activity, (3) compaction-aware transcript extraction discards stale pre-compaction test results. Two builder branches ready for merge: compaction-builder (implementation + tests, 461 insertions) and compaction-builder-2 (version bump 3.8.0 → 3.9.0).

title: Stop hook session check too strict + last_activity staleness trap
description:
Issue #28: PRISM workflow stuck on step 0 (review_previous_notes) consuming 2.2M tokens. Hooks stop firing because:

1. is_same_session() returns False when session_id is missing from hook input — line 1434 rejects with 'return False' when current_session_id is empty but stored_session is set. Fix: return True (lenient) when current_session_id is empty, falling through to the staleness check for protection.

2. last_activity only updates conditionally (if transcript has tokens OR branch changed). If neither condition is met, last_activity stays at its old value and the workflow becomes permanently stale after 2 hours. Fix: always update last_activity unconditionally right after the staleness check passes.

3. build_agent_instruction() fallback not wrapped in try/except — if the Conductor import fails AND the fallback instruction building throws (e.g., missing core-steps file), the exception propagates to the top-level handler and the hook silently exits without advancing. Fix: wrap fallback in try/except with a minimal proceed instruction.

Files: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_stop_hook.py (3 fixes), plugins/prism-devtools/tests/test_prism_stop_hook.py (tests for lenient session check, unconditional last_activity, fallback resilience)
GitHub: https://github.com/siegeon/.prism/issues/28
closeReason: Fixed 3 bugs in prism_stop_hook.py: (1) is_same_session returns True when current_session_id empty (lenient), (2) last_activity updated unconditionally after staleness check, (3) build_agent_instruction fallback wrapped in try/except in both _emit_current_step_reinstruct and main flow. 8 new tests added. Version bumped 3.9.0->3.9.1.

title: Stop hook emits massive instruction in block decision on every turn — write to file instead
description:
Issue #29: The stop hook injects the full step instruction (3-5KB) directly into the block decision reason field on every Stop event. This text enters the conversation context and accumulates, burning tokens. User sees 'same massive message over and over.'

ROOT CAUSE: build_agent_instruction() output (~3-5KB: role card + inline rules + conventions + core-step body + skills discovery + brain context + memory instruction + stop directive) is placed directly in the block decision reason field. Every time Claude responds, the Stop hook fires and either:
1. Re-emits the instruction via no-progress detection
2. Re-emits via validation failure continue_instruction
3. Emits the next step instruction at transition

All three paths inject kilobytes of text into the conversation context.

ARCHITECTURAL FIX — Write instruction to file, reference in block:

1. At step transitions, write the full instruction to .prism/current_instruction.md
2. Block decision becomes SHORT: '[PRISM] Step 4/8: write_failing_tests. Your full instruction is at .prism/current_instruction.md — read it now and begin.'
3. For validation failures, block with JUST the specific failure: '[PRISM] Tests not failing yet. Continue working on write_failing_tests.'
4. For no-progress re-engagement, block with: '[PRISM] Continue current step: write_failing_tests. Read .prism/current_instruction.md if needed.'

This reduces each block decision from ~3KB to ~150 bytes. The instruction is read ONCE per step (when Claude reads the file), not injected on every turn.

IMPLEMENTATION in prism_stop_hook.py:
- Add _write_instruction_file(instruction, step_id, project_root) that writes to .prism/current_instruction.md
- At step transition (line ~2017): call _write_instruction_file before printing block
- Change block reason from full instruction to short pointer
- Change _emit_current_step_reinstruct to use short message (not full instruction)
- Change validation failure blocks to use short messages
- Ensure .prism/current_instruction.md is cleaned up by cleanup()

Files: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_stop_hook.py, plugins/prism-devtools/tests/test_prism_stop_hook.py
GitHub: https://github.com/siegeon/.prism/issues/29
closeReason: Stop hook instruction-to-file: full 3-5KB instruction now written to .prism/current_instruction.md at step transitions instead of injected into block decisions. Block reasons are short pointers (<300 chars). Re-engagement messages are short nudges. cleanup() removes the file. Version 3.9.1→3.9.2. 114 tests passing (10 new).

title: Hooks fail on Windows — python3 not found + add cross-platform hook runner
description:
Issue #30 (third report of same root cause from #25 and #28): PRISM hooks fail to fire consistently on Windows because hooks.json uses python3 which may not be available. On Windows, Python is typically installed as python, not python3.

Evidence: 3 separate bug reports all showing hooks not firing on Windows, agent stuck at step 0, last_activity never updated. User frustrated: 'third time we have this bug.'

FIX: Add a cross-platform hook runner script and update hooks.json.

1. Create hooks/run-hook.sh:
   #\!/bin/sh
   # Cross-platform Python resolver for PRISM hooks
   PY=$(command -v python3 2>/dev/null || command -v python 2>/dev/null || echo python3)
   exec "$PY" "$@"

2. Update ALL commands in hooks.json from:
   python3 ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/<script>.py
   TO:
   sh ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.sh ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/<script>.py

3. Also update setup_prism_loop.py to write the first step instruction to .prism/current_instruction.md at setup time (not just print it). This way even if the stop hook doesn't fire, the instruction file exists for Claude to read.

Files: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/run-hook.sh (NEW), plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/hooks.json (update all commands), plugins/prism-devtools/skills/prism-loop/scripts/setup_prism_loop.py (write instruction file at setup)
GitHub: https://github.com/siegeon/.prism/issues/30
closeReason: Added cross-platform hook runner (run-hook.sh) that resolves python3||python at runtime. Updated all 12 hooks.json commands to use the runner. Added instruction file write at setup time in setup_prism_loop.py as fallback for when stop hook doesn't fire. Fixes root cause of 3 Windows bug reports (issues #25, #28, #30).

title: Improve prism-bug diagnostic skill with platform & hook execution diagnostics
description:
The prism-bug skill (/prism-bug) captures session state but misses the root cause diagnostics that would identify hook execution failures. After 4-5 iterations of the same Windows hooks-not-firing bug, the reports always show symptoms (no hook_progress, module import failures) but never capture WHY hooks don't fire.

Add these diagnostic sections to prism-bug.py:

1. **Platform Diagnostics section** — OS name/version, Python executable path, command availability (python3/python/sh/bash with which/where), current shell.

2. **Hook Execution Test section** — Read hooks.json, try running at least one hook command (e.g. session-start with empty JSON input), report exit code + stderr. Also verify run-hook.sh exists and works.

3. **Hook Script Verification** — For each command in hooks.json, verify the referenced script path exists. Report missing scripts.

4. **Transcript System Events** — Scan transcript for system role messages containing 'hook' (hook errors, hook lifecycle events), not just hook_progress.

5. **hooks.json Content** — Include the actual hooks.json commands in the report so we can see what's configured.

title: Remove sh/run-hook.sh wrapper: use python3 directly in hooks.json
description:
GitHub issue #32: Users on native Windows getting PostToolUse:Bash hook errors. Root cause: hooks.json uses 'sh ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.sh <script>.py' which spawns sh.exe (Git Bash) -> python3 -> script.py. This 3-process chain breaks on Windows paths with backslashes.

Fix: Change ALL hook commands in hooks.json from:
  sh ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/run-hook.sh ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/<script>.py
to:
  python3 ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/hooks/<script>.py

This eliminates the sh wrapper entirely. python3 is available on all target platforms (Windows 3.11+, WSL, Linux, macOS). The run-hook.sh file can remain for backward compat but is no longer referenced.

File area: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/hooks.json, plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/run-hook.sh

title: Conductor-driven skill selection: filter skills per step using usage data
description:
The Conductor optimizes prompt variants (PSP) and brain context, but skill injection is completely static — all 26 skills dumped with MANDATORY into every step. We already collect skill_usage data in Brain (record_skill_usage), but never query it to filter.

Needed:
1. Conductor.select_relevant_skills(step_id, agent, all_skills) -> filtered list of 3-5 skills
2. Query skill_usage + session_outcomes tables to score which skills actually helped per step/persona
3. Cold-start heuristic: when no usage data exists, use step-to-skill mapping (e.g. write_failing_tests -> test, blackbox; implement_tasks -> api, db, domain, patterns)
4. _format_discovered_skills() called with filtered list, not full list
5. Change MANDATORY language to 'Consider these relevant skills' for filtered set
6. Conductor.build_agent_instruction() passes filtered skills to _base() instead of letting _base() discover independently

Key files:
- plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/conductor_engine.py (add select_relevant_skills)
- plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_loop_context.py (accept filtered skills param, stop calling discover_prism_skills internally when Conductor provides them)
- plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/brain_engine.py (may need query method for skill_usage aggregation)

Acceptance: Skills injected per step are filtered to top 3-5 relevant ones. Full 26-skill dump eliminated. Cold-start mapping works when no usage data. Tests verify filtering. Version bump.
closeReason: Conductor-driven skill filtering implemented: select_relevant_skills() filters all 26 skills to top 3-5 per step using Brain usage data + cold-start keyword heuristics. prism_loop_context accepts filtered_skills param with softer Consider language. Version bumped to 3.10.4. 13 new tests.

title: Gate bypass: agent self-approves green_gate by invoking /prism-approve skill
description:
GitHub issue #34: Agent autonomously invoked /prism-approve during green_gate without waiting for user. The gate step should set paused_for_manual:true and STOP, requiring explicit user action. But with skills injected, the agent sees /checkin and /prism-approve as available tools and invokes them.

Two fixes needed:
1. Gate steps (red_gate, green_gate) must NOT have approve/reject skills in their filtered skill list. The Conductor's select_relevant_skills cold-start mapping for gates should exclude checkin, task, and any workflow-control skills.
2. The gate step instructions should explicitly say DO NOT invoke /prism-approve -- wait for the user.

Also: Brain system_context() returning 0 results with 770 docs. And Conductor epsilon format error: 'unsupported format string passed to method.__format__'. These need investigation.

File area: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/conductor_engine.py, plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_loop_context.py, plugins/prism-devtools/core-steps/red_gate.md, plugins/prism-devtools/core-steps/green_gate.md
closeReason: Fixed gate bypass: agent can no longer self-approve at gate steps. Three-layer defense: (1) empty keyword lists in _STEP_SKILL_KEYWORDS for gate steps, (2) early return in select_relevant_skills(), (3) LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS membership. Gate messages now include explicit DO NOT INVOKE warnings. Also: defensive float() cast for conductor epsilon format error, brain system_context() diagnostic logging when 0 results with indexed docs, is_same_session() bug fix aligning with existing test. Version bump 3.10.5 → 3.10.6.

title: prism-bug diagnostic: preserve state file and step_history after workflow completion
description: GitHub issue #34 showed diagnostic gaps: state file is cleaned up on workflow completion so filing a bug post-completion loses step_history, gate results, and other state data. The prism-bug tool should archive the state file contents before cleanup, or the stop hook should preserve a .prism/last_session_state.yaml snapshot. This ensures post-mortem diagnostics always have the full step history.

title: Brain search: FTS5 query too long + RRF threshold too high for degraded mode
description:
GitHub issue #35: Brain system_context() returns 0 results despite 800 indexed docs. TWO bugs in brain_engine.py:

BUG A — FTS5 query too long (brain_engine.py:1443-1468): system_context() reads first 1000 chars of story file and passes directly as FTS5 MATCH query. SQLite FTS5 uses implicit AND for multi-token queries. ~150 tokens from story content means FTS5 requires ALL 150 tokens present in a single document — virtually never matches. Fix: extract top ~10 key terms using TF-IDF-style extraction or OR logic in the FTS5 query. Could use FTS5 OR syntax: 'term1 OR term2 OR term3'.

BUG B — RRF threshold too high (brain_engine.py:1692): rrf_score >= 0.02 filter is mathematically impossible when only one search index contributes. With k=60, max single-list score = 1/61 = 0.0164 < 0.02. When vector_enabled=False AND graph returns empty, only FTS5 contributes. Fix: make threshold adaptive — lower to 0.01 when fewer than 3 indexes contribute, or compute threshold as 1/(k+1) minus small margin.

Acceptance: (1) FTS5 queries are capped at ~10-15 key terms using OR logic. (2) RRF threshold adapts to number of contributing indexes. (3) system_context() returns relevant results for a story file with 800 docs indexed. (4) Existing Brain tests pass.
closeReason: Fixed two bugs in brain_engine.py: (1) FTS5 query too long — added _extract_fts_query() to cap at 12 key terms with OR logic, (2) RRF threshold too high — made adaptive based on vector_enabled (0.02 vs 0.0148). 402 Python tests pass.

title: Skill invocation: Conductor-filtered language too soft, agents skip all skills
description:
GitHub issue #35: Despite 28 skills discovered and injected into agent instructions, agents invoke 0 skills (step_skill_calls=0 across all 6 workflow steps). Root cause: when Conductor filtering is active, _format_discovered_skills() uses 'Consider these relevant skills' (prism_loop_context.py:337) instead of 'MANDATORY: You MUST invoke' (line 339). The agent treats 'Consider' as optional and skips everything.

Fix: change the filtered skill instruction language to be more directive. Instead of 'Consider these relevant skills for this step. Invoke any that apply:', use stronger language like 'You MUST check and invoke these skills before completing your task. These have been selected as relevant to this step:' — firm but not as aggressive as the MANDATORY all-caps version used for unfiltered mode.

Also verify: the skill list IS actually being included in the instruction output (not suppressed by LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS or another guard). The 3 non-lightweight steps (write_failing_tests, implement_tasks, verify_green_state) should all have skills in their instructions.

Acceptance: (1) Filtered skill language is directive enough that agents consistently invoke relevant skills. (2) LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS still correctly suppress skills for review_previous_notes, verify_plan, red_gate, green_gate. (3) Existing skill filtering tests pass.
closeReason: Changed filtered skill instruction language from soft 'Consider these relevant skills' to directive 'You MUST check and invoke these skills before completing your task'. Updated tests in test_skill_filtering.py. All 95 tests pass.

title: Phase-aware skill matching: priority ranges, agent roles, scored ranking
description:
select_relevant_skills() produces identical 5/33 results for every step because:
1. It ignores the agent parameter (sm/qa/dev)
2. No phase metadata — customer skills encode phase in priority ranges but Conductor doesn't use them
3. Substring matching is too broad: 'design' matches 7 BUILD skills, polluting draft_story results

FIX: Replace boolean substring matching with scored, phase-aware ranking.

ARCHITECTURE:

Layer 1 — Priority-range phase detection:
Customer skills encode phase in priority ranges:
  10-19 = top-level (always-available)
  20-29 = BUILD phase
  30-39 = VERIFY phase
  40-49 = SHIP phase
  50-59 = OPERATE phase
Add _infer_skill_phase(skill) that maps priority to phase.
Map steps to expected phases:
  write_failing_tests → VERIFY
  implement_tasks → BUILD
  draft_story → PLANNING (top-level only)
  verify_plan → VERIFY + top-level
  verify_green_state → VERIFY
  review_previous_notes → top-level only

Layer 2 — Agent/persona matching:
select_relevant_skills already receives 'agent' (sm/qa/dev).
Add _STEP_AGENT_MAP or use STEP_PHASE_MAP to determine persona.
Skills with agent field set should be preferred when agent matches.
Skills with agent=null are phase-generic (match any persona).

Layer 3 — Scored ranking:
Replace boolean substring matching with a scoring function:
  _score_skill(skill, step_id, agent, keywords) -> float
  - name exact match to a keyword:   +10
  - name contains keyword:           +5
  - description contains keyword:    +3
  - skill phase matches step phase:  +8 (CRITICAL — this is the main differentiator)
  - skill agent matches step agent:  +4
  - top-level skill (priority 10-80): +2 (always somewhat relevant)
  - Brain usage score (if available): +usage_count (additive, overrides cold-start when high)

Sort all skills by score descending, return top max_skills.

EXPECTED JOURNEY RESULTS AFTER FIX:

SM Journey:
  review_previous_notes → review, task, checkin, audit (top-level/context, phase boost blocks BUILD/VERIFY)
  draft_story → task, review, checkin (top-level planning, NOT api/db/domain)
  verify_plan → validate, verify, review, task, test (VERIFY phase + top-level)

QA Journey:
  write_failing_tests → blackbox, test-design, e2e-test, integration-test, test (VERIFY phase skills!)
  verify_green_state → validate, coverage, regression, smoke-test, verify (VERIFY phase skills!)

DEV Journey:
  implement_tasks → api, db, domain, query, patterns (BUILD phase skills!)

FILES TO MODIFY:
- hooks/conductor_engine.py: _infer_skill_phase(), _score_skill(), update select_relevant_skills()
- hooks/prism_loop_context.py: update _parse_skill_frontmatter to also extract 'phase' from prism: block (optional)
- tests/harness/fixtures/customer-skills.jsonl: add 'phase' field to skills (optional, derived from priority if absent)
- tests/test_customer_skill_lifecycle.py: update journey assertions to validate differentiated results
- tests/conftest.py: update report to show differentiation

CRITICAL CONSTRAINT: The fix must work WITHOUT requiring customers to change their SKILL.md files. Priority-range detection is the primary mechanism. Explicit phase/agent metadata is an enhancement.

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
AC-1: implement_tasks returns ≥4 BUILD-phase skills (api, db, domain, query, patterns) from 33-skill pool
AC-2: write_failing_tests returns ≥4 VERIFY-phase skills (blackbox, test-design, e2e-test, etc.) from 33-skill pool
AC-3: draft_story returns 0 BUILD sub-skills — no api, db, domain in result
AC-4: draft_story returns ≥2 top-level skills (task, review, etc.)
AC-5: verify_green_state returns ≥4 VERIFY-phase skills
AC-6: review_previous_notes returns 0 BUILD and 0 VERIFY sub-skills
AC-7: Gate steps still return []
AC-8: Brain ranking still overrides when usage data available
AC-9: All existing tests in test_skill_filtering.py still pass (backward compat with small skill sets)
AC-10: Journey test — SM sees different skills at each step transition
AC-11: Journey test — QA write_failing_tests vs verify_green_state have overlapping but not identical results
AC-12: Journey test — DEV implement_tasks has zero overlap with QA write_failing_tests top 5
closeReason: Implemented phase-aware skill matching: _infer_skill_phase maps priority ranges to lifecycle phases, _score_skill provides additive scoring (phase+keyword+brain+tiebreak), select_relevant_skills unified to single scored ranking path. 117 tests pass including 7 new prism-2140 AC tests.

title: Multi-turn lifecycle harness: measure Brain learning effectiveness across features
description:
The skill matching system always returns exactly 5 skills per step regardless of learning history. Brain usage data is global (not per-step), so after 10 sessions the system still returns the same 5/33 for every phase. The system isn't actually getting smarter.

THREE PROBLEMS TO FIX:

1. BRAIN LEARNING IS NOT PER-STEP
   get_skill_scores() returns global usage counts. If DEV uses 'api' 50 times at implement_tasks, that score boosts 'api' equally for draft_story and write_failing_tests. Need per-step (or per-phase) usage tracking so the Brain learns WHICH skills are useful WHERE.

2. ALWAYS RETURNS EXACTLY max_skills (5)
   No score threshold or confidence cutoff. A skill scoring 0.01 gets returned alongside one scoring 13.0. After learning, the system should return fewer skills when confident (3 highly-relevant > 5 mediocre), or more when uncertain.

3. NO MULTI-TURN TEST HARNESS
   Current tests are single-turn: set up skills, call select_relevant_skills once, check results. No test simulates building Feature A, recording brain data, then building Feature B and measuring improvement.

DELIVERABLE: Multi-turn lifecycle harness test

Create test_multi_turn_lifecycle.py that simulates 2 feature builds:

Turn 1 — Feature A (cold start):
  - SM: draft_story → record which skills matched
  - DEV: implement_tasks → simulate using api, db, domain → record brain usage
  - QA: write_failing_tests → simulate using e2e-test, integration-test → record brain usage
  - QA: verify_green_state → simulate using validate, coverage → record brain usage
  - Capture cold-start results as baseline

Turn 2 — Feature B (with learning):
  - SM: draft_story → skills should reflect learning (task/review ranked higher from Turn 1)
  - DEV: implement_tasks → api/db/domain should rank higher than cold-start
  - QA: write_failing_tests → e2e-test/integration-test should outrank cold-start
  - Measure improvement vs baseline

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
AC-1: Brain records per-step (or per-phase) skill usage, not just global counts
AC-2: select_relevant_skills returns different results after Turn 1 vs cold-start for at least 3 steps
AC-3: DEV implement_tasks in Turn 2 has api/db/domain ranked higher than Turn 1
AC-4: QA write_failing_tests in Turn 2 has test-design/e2e-test ranked higher than Turn 1
AC-5: Skill count per step can vary (not always 5) — use score threshold or confidence cutoff
AC-6: Conftest report shows learning trajectory: cold-start column vs learned column with delta
AC-7: All existing tests still pass (backward compat)

FILES:
- hooks/conductor_engine.py: per-step brain scoring, dynamic max_skills
- hooks/brain.py: per-step/per-phase skill_usage tracking (new table or extended schema)
- tests/test_multi_turn_lifecycle.py: new multi-turn harness
- tests/conftest.py: enhanced report showing learning delta
closeReason: Multi-turn lifecycle harness complete. 2 builders: (1) engine-changes-builder: per-step skill_usage tracking in brain_engine.py + SCORE_CONFIDENCE_RATIO variable count in conductor_engine.py, (2) test-harness-builder: test_multi_turn_lifecycle.py with 9 tests + conftest.py learning trajectory report. Both merge_ready sent to coordinator.

title: Fix skill usage tracking and Brain search returning 0 results (issue #36)
description:
GitHub issue #36: skill_calls always shows 1, brain_queries always shows 0.

TWO ROOT CAUSES:

1. SKILL USAGE NOT RECORDED BY NAME PER STEP
The stop hook counts Skill tool_use blocks (prism_stop_hook.py:1107) but doesn't extract WHICH skill was invoked. step_history stores s:1 (count) but not the skill name. record_outcome() gets metrics dict with skill_calls count but no skill names. Brain.skill_usage table has skill_name + step_id columns but nothing writes to them during normal workflow execution.

FIX: In get_usage_from_transcript(), when a Skill tool_use block is found, extract the skill name from the tool input (block.get("input",{}).get("skill","")). Return skill_names list alongside skill_calls count. In the stop hook, after counting, call brain.record_skill_usage(skill_name, step_id, session_id) for each skill used. This feeds the learning loop.

2. BRAIN system_context() RETURNS 0 RESULTS WITH 810 DOCS
Bug report shows Brain has 810 indexed docs but system_context() returns 0. The RRF threshold filter (brain_engine.py:1756) rejects all results. With only FTS5 active (vector_enabled=False on most installs), the adaptive threshold at line 1755 may still be too high, or FTS5 tokenization doesn't match the query terms from the story file content.

Likely cause: system_context() reads first 1000 chars of story file as query (brain_engine.py:1722). Story files contain markdown with acceptance criteria — long, complex text that FTS5 doesn't match well against code file contents. The RRF scores fall below threshold.

FIX: Improve system_context() query construction — extract key terms from story rather than dumping raw markdown. Also lower the RRF threshold floor or add a fallback that returns top-N results regardless of score when strict filtering returns empty.

FILES:
- hooks/prism_stop_hook.py: extract skill names from transcript, record to brain
- hooks/brain_engine.py: fix system_context query construction, fix RRF threshold
- hooks/conductor_engine.py: wire skill name recording through record_outcome

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
AC-1: get_usage_from_transcript returns skill_names list with actual skill names used
AC-2: stop hook records each skill name to brain.skill_usage with step_id
AC-3: system_context() returns >0 results when Brain has 810+ docs and story file exists
AC-4: step_history bq field shows >0 after a step where Brain had indexed content
AC-5: All existing tests pass
AC-6: New tests verify skill name extraction from transcript and brain query non-zero
closeReason: Fixed both root causes: skill name extraction from transcript + brain FTS query term extraction with RRF fallback. 13 new tests, all 134 pass.

title: TUI header ●SFR label counts all subagent runs, not just SFR
description: app.py line 186-190: _sfr_total = len(rows) counts ALL subagent_outcomes rows (SFR + freeform), but the TUI header labels it ●SFR {total}r. When all 91 runs are freeform (prompt_id has no /sfr), the header shows ●SFR 91r — misleading. Fix: show ●SFR 0/91r or ●RUNS 91 (0 SFR) to distinguish. The snapshot.py CLI already handles this correctly at line 588.
closeReason: Fixed TUI header SFR label to show sfr_runs/total instead of total-only. Fixed subagent_stop_recorder to read prompt_id from per-agent variant file.

title: subagent_stop_recorder writes empty prompt_id instead of {namespace}/freeform
description: subagent_stop_recorder.py isn't capturing the prompt_id from the conductor injection hook. _select_subagent_variant() returns (namespace/freeform, '') but the prompt_id stored in subagent_outcomes is empty string ''. This causes _read_sfr_stats() to classify all runs as freeform (correct) but also means variant selection analytics are lost — no way to track which runs used which variant. Fix: ensure the prompt_id flows from conductor injection through to subagent_stop_recorder recording.
closeReason: Fixed prompt_id path mismatch: subagent_stop_recorder now reads from .prism/brain/subagent_variants/{safe_name} matching conductor._save_subagent_prompt_id() write path.

title: Stop hook 'Story file not found' when implement_tasks runs >1h after draft_story
description: Root cause: detect_story_file() uses 1-hour recency window (line 1731). Long sessions reliably exceed this. Three compounding issues: (1) 1-hour threshold too short — story file >1h old is not found. (2) track-current-story.py writes .prism-current-story.txt but detect_story_file() never reads it — the PostToolUse capture is wasted. (3) story_file detection only runs after draft_story step (line 1980) — if missed there, never retried. Fix: (a) detect_story_file() should check .prism-current-story.txt first, (b) extend threshold to 24h or remove it entirely, (c) retry story detection at every step transition if story_file is still blank.
closeReason: Fixed: detect_story_file() now checks tracker file first, uses 24h window, retries at every step transition

title: Stop hook resilience: default IGNORED dirs for security scan, configurable test timeout
description: From issue #37 item 2. Security scan in validate_step() should ship default IGNORED directories (node_modules, .git, bin, obj, .playwright, storybook-static) so it doesn't flag build artifacts. Test timeout should be configurable via state file or env var rather than hardcoded. Story file detection already fixed in prism-a177.
closeReason: Promoted security scan IGNORED dirs to module-level constant, added configurable test timeout via state file/env var, archived state to last_session_state.yaml before cleanup. 16 new tests, all 133 passing.

title: Auto-commit at phase boundaries to prevent context compaction data loss
description: From issue #37 item 3. When sessions run long (287min for TP-117), context compaction can lose uncommitted work. The stop hook should auto-commit staged changes at phase boundaries (after green_gate approval, after verify_green_state). This preserves work even if the session compacts or crashes. Implementation: add git add + git commit in the step transition logic when crossing phase boundaries.
closeReason: Implemented auto-commit at phase boundaries. _auto_commit_phase_boundary() in prism_stop_hook.py runs git add -A + git commit before gate pauses (write_failing_tests->red_gate, verify_green_state->green_gate). Also called in prism_approve.py at green_gate approval. 5 new tests. All 606 tests pass.

title: Sizing-aware workflow: lightweight path for R (Routine) stories
description: From issue #37 items 1 and 5. Routine mechanical tasks (config changes, model upgrades, flag flips) should not go through the full 8-step ceremony. Add a complexity-class heuristic: R=Routine (skip verify_plan, reduce QA), M=Medium (standard), L=Large (full ceremony). Could key off story size field, keyword detection in prompt, or explicit /prism-start --size R flag. Affects setup_prism_loop.py step list and prism_stop_hook.py step skipping.

title: QA agent should detect integration test infrastructure (Docker/Aspire) before deferring
description: From issue #37 item 4. QA agent deferred AC-5 integration tests for TP-117 because it assumed no test infrastructure. But TalentSyncPro has Docker+Aspire integration test setup. The conductor instruction for write_failing_tests should detect available test infrastructure (check for docker-compose.yml, .dcproj, AppHost project, Playwright config) and include it in the QA agent's context so it doesn't falsely defer.

title: Fix stop hook: security scanner IGNORED dirs, test timeout, file size guard (issue #38)
description:
Five fixes in prism_stop_hook.py per GitHub issue #38:

1. IGNORED set (line 277) missing: bin, obj, .vs, .docusaurus, .serena, .context, .prism, NDependOut, storybook-static, wwwroot, TestResults, coverage, .playwright. Scanner walks into build artifacts causing false positives.

2. Security scanner uses cwd.glob('**/*ext') (line 315) which walks all dirs before _skip filters. Switch to os.walk with early pruning — skip IGNORED dirs before descending.

3. Add file size guard — skip files >100KB to avoid scanning minified JS bundles that trigger eval/password regex false positives.

4. run_tests() timeout hardcoded 30s (line 229). Too short for dotnet test on full solutions. Make configurable: check PRISM_TEST_TIMEOUT env var, fall back to 120s default.

5. green_full validation runs redundant test suite and security scan (already done by skills). Add skip_redundant_checks flag — if transcript shows tests already passed in this step, skip re-running.

File area: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_stop_hook.py only.
closeReason: All 5 fixes merged: expanded IGNORED dirs, os.walk pruning, 100KB file size guard, configurable test timeout, skip redundant security scan

title: Brain returns stale/duplicate results across PRISM loop steps (issue #39)
description: Brain system_context() returns identical results for draft_story and verify_plan because both query with similar story content. Four problems: (1) No step-aware query differentiation — system_context() doesn't factor in current step_id, so all steps get the same results. (2) Query terms derived from story file are too similar across early steps — both draft_story and verify_plan use same story content. (3) Brain context injection in build_agent_instruction() at conductor_engine.py passes story_file to system_context() but doesn't pass step_id for step-aware retrieval. Fix: Pass step_id to system_context(), use step-specific query augmentation (e.g. add step keywords to query), and deduplicate results against what was returned in previous steps (store last_brain_docs per step in state).
closeReason: Merged: step-aware query keywords, step_id passthrough, result deduplication

title: Skills not invoked during GREEN phase — move skills section, add enforcement (issue #40)
description: Skills are listed at the bottom of the instruction (line 558-559 in prism_loop_context.py) after body, inline rules, and retrieval instruction. Agent ignores them because by the time it reaches the skill list, it's already deep into manual work. Two fixes: (1) Move the Available Skills section to near the top of the instruction, right after the role card and before the core step body. (2) In step instructions like implement_tasks.md and verify_green_state.md, replace generic 'Check for available skills' with specific directives: 'Run /test to verify' or 'Use /checkin for commit'. The MUST language exists but is too far down to be effective.
closeReason: Merged: skills moved to top of instruction, step-specific skill directives in core-steps

title: Auto-detect story size in review_previous_notes, skip steps for Routine work
description:
PRISM should auto-detect story complexity during review_previous_notes and dynamically skip unnecessary steps for routine/mechanical work.

Design:
1. Add _classify_story_size(prompt, brain) function in prism_stop_hook.py that returns R/M/L:
   - R signals: 'add field', 'rename', 'config change', 'bump version', 'update prompt', 'thread through', single-entity changes, <3 files likely touched
   - M signals: default — standard feature work
   - L signals: 'new subsystem', 'redesign', 'migration', 'multi-service', 'new API surface', 'architecture'
   - Brain augmentation: query past stories with similar prompts, use their actual duration/token data to calibrate
   
2. Store story_size in state file (new field). Set during review_previous_notes → draft_story transition.

3. Add _SKIP_STEPS_FOR_SIZE mapping:
   - R: skip verify_plan, skip red_gate (go draft_story → write_failing_tests → implement_tasks → verify_green_state → green_gate)
   - M: standard 8-step flow
   - L: standard 8-step flow (could add extra review steps later)

4. In the step transition logic (around line 1964), check story_size and skip steps in the skip set. Increment next_index past skipped steps.

5. review_previous_notes.md should output the detected size in its summary so the user can see it and override if needed.

File area: plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_stop_hook.py (sizing logic + step skipping), plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/core-steps/review_previous_notes.md (output format), plugins/prism-devtools/hooks/prism_loop_context.py (LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS may need updating)

Acceptance:
- Prompt 'add a field to the API response' → classified R, skips verify_plan
- Prompt 'design a new multi-tenant billing subsystem' → classified L, full ceremony
- story_size stored in state file, visible in CLI
- Step transitions skip appropriately for R-sized stories
- Existing tests pass + new tests for classification heuristics
closeReason: Merged: auto-detect R/M/L story size, R skips verify_plan+red_gate, 28 new tests

title: CLI dashboard not showing token counts (issue #41)
description: CLI TUI renders but token usage is empty/missing. The _read_live_tokens() method in app.py (line 215) locates the transcript via Path.home()/.claude/projects/*/SESSION_ID.jsonl glob. Possible causes on Windows: (1) Path.home() returns C:\Users\siege but transcript is under a different path on Windows, (2) glob pattern with * may not match the project-encoded path, (3) session_id from state file may be empty or mismatched. Need to add diagnostic logging and fix the transcript path resolution for Windows. Also check that total_tokens is displayed when state.total_tokens > 0 (line 152-153 in app.py format_title).
closeReason: Merged: centralized transcript path resolution with Windows/WSL compat, ? tok fallback, 3 new tests

title: BYOS skill bypass: agents run raw commands instead of invoking customer skills
description: Customer reports dashboard shows Skills: 0 because agents run dotnet test / dotnet build directly via Bash instead of using /test, /build skills. The BYOS instruction says MUST invoke but doesn't explicitly prohibit running equivalent commands directly. Two fixes: (1) _format_discovered_skills() in prism_loop_context.py must add explicit DO NOT language — for each skill, tell the agent what raw command it replaces and forbid running it directly. (2) Stop hook enforcement in prism_stop_hook.py — after a step, scan Bash tool_use blocks for commands matching available skills and log a warning/flag so the agent self-corrects. File area: hooks/prism_loop_context.py (instruction formatting), hooks/prism_stop_hook.py (enforcement validation).
closeReason: Implemented BYOS skill bypass enforcement: (1) _parse_skill_frontmatter extracts optional replaces: field, _format_discovered_skills adds explicit DO NOT run language per skill and in header; (2) detect_skill_bypass() in stop hook scans Bash tool_use transcript blocks for commands matching skill replaces fields, emits warnings at step transitions. 16 new tests, 217 total passing.

The section should: (1) List all available skills from discover_prism_skills(), (2) Extract skill_names actually invoked from transcript (Skill tool_use blocks), (3) Extract Bash commands from transcript tool_use blocks, (4) Compute adoption score (invoked/available %), (5) Show skills NOT invoked, (6) Show Bash commands that ran during the session. title: New /prism-done skill: intentional session completion with metrics recording
description: Users currently cancel/ctrl+c to end PRISM sessions which loses the opportunity to record metrics and learn. Create a /prism-done skill that: (1) Parses session state (steps completed, story file, step_history), (2) Extracts transcript metrics via get_usage_from_transcript (total tokens, duration, skill_names, tool_calls, brain queries), (3) Records session outcome to Brain via record_session_outcome(), (4) Records each skill usage via record_skill_usage(), (5) Prints a session summary report card (steps completed, tokens used, skills invoked, duration, files modified), (6) Offers to commit outstanding work (git add tracked files + git commit), (7) Archives and cleans up state file. title: Fix inflated token counts: stop adding cache_read/cache_creation tokens to total
description: Token counts in PRISM dashboard show millions because get_usage_from_transcript() and TUI app.py add cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens to total_tokens. Cache read tokens are served from cache (90% discount) and re-counted every turn, massively inflating the number. Fix: total_tokens = input_tokens + output_tokens only. Optionally track cache tokens separately. Three locations: (1) prism_stop_hook.py lines 1185-1187, (2) app.py lines 277-280, (3) snapshot.py token parsing. Also update prism-done.py if it copies this pattern.
closeReason: Fixed inflated token counts: removed cache_creation_input_tokens and cache_read_input_tokens from total_tokens calculation across 4 code locations (get_usage_from_transcript, get_session_metrics_from_transcript, app._read_live_tokens, snapshot._inject_live_tokens). Updated 3 test files (6 total files changed). All 72 tests pass.

title: Fix dotnet format lint: use --severity error to ignore warnings
description: GitHub issue #43: dotnet format --verify-no-changes treats warnings as errors, blocking verify_green_state indefinitely. Line 273 of prism_stop_hook.py sets lint command to 'dotnet format --verify-no-changes'. Fix: add --severity error flag so only actual errors block. Also make run_lint() return warnings separately from errors so the validation can show warnings as advisory without blocking.
closeReason: Fixed dotnet format lint: added --severity error flag to prevent warnings from blocking verify_green_state. Updated run_lint() to return advisory warnings separately. 8 new tests added, all 148 tests pass.

title: Add circuit breaker: max retries for verify_green_state to prevent infinite loops
description: GitHub issue #43: PRISM session stuck in 10-hour loop because verify_green_state kept failing on lint warnings. The stop hook has no retry limit — when validation fails, the agent retries indefinitely. Add a circuit breaker: track consecutive validation failures per step in step_history. After N failures (default 3) on the same step, stop blocking and escalate to the user with a message like 'Validation has failed 3 times on the same issue. Manual intervention needed.' This protects all projects regardless of their lint tooling. The counter resets when the validation error message changes (meaning the agent actually fixed something).
closeReason: Added circuit breaker to prism_stop_hook.py to prevent infinite validation loops (GitHub issue #43). Tracks consecutive same-error validation failures per step via step_failure_counts state field. After 3 identical errors, stops blocking and escalates to user. Counter resets when error message changes or validation passes. 8 new tests added, all 149 stop hook tests pass.

title: Make lint/test commands configurable per project instead of hardcoded
description: detect_test_runner() in prism_stop_hook.py hardcodes lint/test commands per project type (dotnet format, ruff, golangci-lint). When the auto-detected command is wrong (e.g. dotnet format warnings treated as errors per issue #43), customers have no way to override it. PRISM should be agnostic. Add a project-level config (e.g. .prism/config.yaml or state file) where customers can set test_command and lint_command. detect_test_runner() checks for overrides first, falls back to auto-detection. This lets each project define exactly how to run tests and lint for their stack.

title: Extend BYOS detection to lint skills and remove hardcoded lint fallback
description: Stop hook _detect_byos_test_skill only detects test skills. Extend to also detect lint skills. When no BYOS lint skill exists return lint=None instead of hardcoding dotnet format or ruff or golangci-lint. Hardcoded lint commands violate PRISM agnostic principle and caused issue 43 ten-hour loop. Accept: 1) detect lint skills from BYOS 2) lint=None when no BYOS lint skill 3) keep test auto-detect as safety net

Removed all hardcoded lint commands from detect_test_runner() (npm run lint, ruff, dotnet format, golangci-lint). lint field now comes from BYOS lint skill or None. 12 new tests added, 161 total pass.

title: Enforce skill usage: block step when skills available but not invoked
description: Root cause of BYOS non-adoption: (1) Core step files contain raw command references like Run test_cmd that compete with skill instructions. (2) detect_skill_bypass is advisory-only, warning on next step instead of blocking current step. (3) validate_step checks outcome (tests pass) but not process (skills used). Fix: A) Remove test_cmd and lint_cmd raw command lines from core step .md files - skills block already tells agent what to do. B) Add skill usage check in validate_step: if discovered skills > 0 and step_skill_calls == 0 and step is not in LIGHTWEIGHT_STEPS, return validation failure telling agent to re-do step using skills. C) Make detect_skill_bypass return blocking validation failure instead of advisory warning prefix. Files: prism_stop_hook.py, core-steps/*.md, prism_loop_context.py
closeReason: Enforced skill usage: (A) removed raw test_cmd/lint_cmd from core-steps/*.md, (B) added skill usage enforcement in validate_step that blocks when skills available but none invoked, (C) made detect_skill_bypass blocking instead of advisory. 6 new tests, 657 total pass.
